answer:
There was one time a few years ago when I really wanted to qualify for the Boston Marathon. I had started getting into running and set that as a big goal for myself. I followed a rigorous training plan and worked hard over several months to build up my mileage and endurance. 

On the day of the qualifying race, I was feeling good and was on track to finish within the qualifying time during the first half. But around mile 15, I hit a wall. My legs just wouldn't go any faster, even though I desperately wanted them to. Over those last agonizing miles, I watched my goal time slowly slip away from me as other runners passed by. 

When I crossed the finish line and realized I had missed the qualifying mark by just a couple of minutes, I was completely crushed-all that training for months only to fall short. I'll admit I wallowed in disappointment over it for a few days. But then I realized that sitting around moping wouldn't make me any faster. If I wanted to achieve my goal, I had to learn from what went wrong and work even harder. 

So, I looked back objectively at my training and race performance. I realized that while I had put in a lot of miles, I hadn't done enough speed work or hill repeats to prepare for the tough course. I also probably went out a little too fast at the start when I should have paced myself better. So I took those lessons and developed a smarter training plan, putting in more hard intervals and tempo runs. 

The next season, I was better prepared both physically and mentally. When race day came, I ran a smart, steady pace and ended up qualifying with time to spare. It just goes to show that you have to keep working and learning from failures to achieve your biggest goals. Not succeeding just means you're not done working yet.
title: Describe a time when you failed to achieve a goal. How did you respond to the failure, and what did you learn from the experience?
extData:
{"answerTemplate":"本题模板:

开场陈述:

开始时回顾你未能达成目标的一个经历: "One instance where I faced failure was when I [briefly describe the situation where you failed to achieve a goal]."

遇到失败的情境:
描述未能达成目标的情况: "In this situation, my goal was to [state the goal], but despite my efforts, [explain what went wrong or why you didn't achieve it]."
强调失败的影响: "The failure impacted me by [describe your initial reaction or feelings about the failure]."

应对失败的方式:
讨论你如何应对这次失败: "In response to this setback, I [describe how you dealt with the failure, such as reflecting on the experience, seeking advice]."
描述从失败中恢复的过程: "To move forward, I [mention specific actions or changes you made, such as setting new goals, adjusting strategies]."

从失败中学到的东西:
讨论从这次经历中学到的教训: "This experience taught me [explain what you learned, such as the importance of resilience, adaptability, or persistence]."
举例说明教训的应用: "For example, since this failure, I have [describe how you have applied these lessons in other situations]."

结论:
总结你对失败和学习的看法: "In conclusion, although not achieving my goal was disheartening, the lessons I learned from this experience have been invaluable."
结束语: "Failure, as much as it hurts, is an important part of learning and growth, providing crucial insights for future endeavors.""}
